← Back to Graphic Design

Comic Book Art

Upload a photo or use the blank canvas, then click and drag to draw. Benjamin Henry Day, Jr. invented a printing process in 1879 which became characteristic of comic books. “Ben Day Dots” are a cheap way to print the illusion of full color. Cyan, magenta, yellow, and key (black) inks were printed in dots on cheap newsprint paper. This tool is inspired by Roy Lichtenstein, the comics of Fletcher Hanks, the animated “Into the Spider-Verse” and, of course, Wonder Woman.

Settings

Maximum file size: 5MB

20

Controls how much detail is captured in the outlines.

2

Reduces texture noise so big object outlines read cleanly.

30

Removes tiny isolated edge fragments.

Upload a photo to start

High-contrast images work best!

Processing Image...

Coloring Tool

50
5

Sets dot diameter. Use Dot Density to change spacing between dots.

70

Drag right for denser dots (darker), left for fewer dots (lighter).

The image is processed locally on your device.

Background

Resilience Continuum is a project by Claire Nyquist which explores science through data visualization, fiction, and graphic design.
